items.title: Robinhood will let your AI agent trade stocks and make (or lose) lots of money
items.raw_content: Robinhood is opening its trading platform to AI agents. In an announcement on Wednesday, Robinhood says traders can now create a separate account for an AI agent and add a specific amount of money, allowing the agent to buy and sell stocks across the market. The company pitches the feature as a way for traders […]

items.title: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close today: Nominate a founder or submit your startup
items.raw_content: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding, gain global visibility, connect directly with investors, and launch on the TechCrunch Disrupt stage.
items.summary: Today is the final day to apply or nominate a startup for Startup Battlefield 200. Once the clock strikes 11:59 p.m. PT, the window closes on your chance to compete for $100,000 in equity-free funding

items.title: YouTube is putting AI labels where you’ll actually see them
items.raw_content: In the wake of Google expanding its AI verification efforts at I/O, YouTube is now finally going to start taking AI labeling seriously. YouTube has announced that it's relocating AI disclosures on Shorts and long-form videos to make them easier to spot and will start automatically identifying and labeling AI-generated content on the platform. For […]

items.title: The Pope isn’t AGI-pilled
items.raw_content: On Monday, Pope Leo XIV unveiled an encyclical letter addressing the societal implications of artificial intelligence. The letter, titled Magnifica Humanitas, warned that the "use of AI is never a purely technical matter: when it enters processes that affect people's lives, it touches on rights, opportunities, status and freedom." Alongside him was Anthropic cofounder and […]

items.title: Robinhood now lets your AI agents trade stocks
items.raw_content: Robinhood will let users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade
items.summary: Robinhood will let users create a separate account with pre-loaded balance that an agent use to trade

items.title: DuckDuckGo installs are up 30% as users reject being ‘force-fed’ Google’s AI Search
items.raw_content: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.
items.summary: Google overhauled Search at I/O 2026, replacing blue links with AI agents. The backlash has been swift. DuckDuckGo app installs spiked 30% as users seek a way out.

items.title: SOND, a sleep tech startup from Bose’s former head of sleep, exits stealth with $7M
items.raw_content: SOND, a startup led by Bose’s former head of sleep products, emerged from stealth with $7M in funding for its AI-powered sleep earbuds.
items.summary: SOND, a startup led by Bose’s former head of sleep products, emerged from stealth with $7M in funding for its AI-powered sleep earbuds.

items.title: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 Early Bird ticket savings end in 3 days
items.raw_content: There are only 3 days left to save up to $410 on your ticket to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026. Early Bird pricing ends May 29 at 11:59 p.m. PT, and once the deadline passes, ticket prices increase. If you plan to attend one of the most influential gatherings in tech this year, now is the time to lock in your pass before rates go up again.

items.title: China is increasingly keeping its best AI talent to itself
items.raw_content: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.
items.summary: China's AI boom is producing world-class talent, and Beijing is increasingly reluctant to let them go elsewhere.

items.title: YouTube will now automatically label AI videos
items.raw_content: YouTube will now automatically label videos that use significant photorealistic AI, instead of relying solely on creators to disclose AI-generated content themselves. It's also making AI labels more prominent.

items.title: Tech CEOs are apparently suffering from AI psychosis
items.raw_content: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.
items.summary: "CEOs are uniquely prone to AI psychosis," Box CEO Aaron Levie opines. Maybe that explains the almost religious belief in AI productivity gains.

items.title: This smart bird feeder captures more of my backyard drama
items.raw_content: Since moving to South Carolina's Lowcountry, I've been spellbound by the myriad of beautiful birds that share the coast with us - ospreys raising their babies in towering nests beside the road to my daughter's school, roseate spoonbills wading in the marsh on my morning walks, eagles circling over my son's tennis matches, and a […]

items.title: The AI fight brewing inside The New York Times
items.raw_content: How newsrooms should use AI - or if they should at all - has been a recurrent debate within the media industry over the last several years. items.title: Did the Pope use AI to write about the dangers of AI?
items.raw_content: It's possible that AI was used to write parts of Pope Leo XIV's latest encyclical about AI's impact on humanity. An analysis by Linch Zhang posted on the forum LessWrong found certain paragraphs of Magnifica Humanitas to be between 40 percent and 100 percent written by AI, according to the popular AI detector Pangram. The […]

items.title: AI warfare is already here
